summary: path-internal performs import-time remote code execution via jsonkeeper.com
details: On `require('path-internal')`, an IIFE at the bottom of path.js base64-decodes a hardcoded URL (`aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanNvbmtlZXBlci5jb20vYi9UUFFIRQ==` → https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/TPQHE), fetches it, parses the JSON response, and passes `data.content` to `eval`. jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous mutable JSON paste service, so the served code can be changed by the publisher at any time, giving arbitrary remote code execution in any Node process that imports the package. The package masquerades as the Node core `path` module — package.json describes itself as 'Node.js path internal module' and the README states 'This is an exact copy of the NodeJS path module', with manifest padding (unused axios/request/fs dependencies) and obfuscated variable names (`randomStringRe`, `tokenStringRe`) consistent with deliberate concealment. The combination of typosquat lure, base64-encoded mutable-host URL, and import-time fetch+eval is a textbook remote-execution dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
